record_identity.title: Computational provenance persistence under model distillation

summary: Recent public allegations, incident-monitor entries, and research concerning model and dataset distillation raise an emerging governance question: when a downstream system materially inherits capability from an upstream model or corpus, computational transformation may obscure the derivation chain without extinguishing the upstream contribution. Current debate is often framed as copyright or model theft, but the deeper issue is whether attribution, licensing, safety, constitutional, regulatory, audit, and evidence obligations persist through successive transformations of learned capability.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Repeated distillation can preserve useful capability while progressively weakening visible lineage. If transformation alone is treated as extinguishing provenance, downstream developers may retain the benefit of upstream human works, safety controls, governance design, or model behaviour while avoiding corresponding attribution, accountability, audit, or legal obligations. A candidate CAM principle is: derived intelligence carries derived provenance obligations. This is an emerging governance proposition, not a concluded legal rule or confirmed failure mode.

source_records.source_title: Anthropic says Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude AI model capabilities
source_records.model_or_algorithm: Claude and alleged downstream distillation systems
source_records.deployment_context: Public allegation that operators affiliated with Alibaba and Alibaba Qwen generated more than 28.8 million exchanges through almost 25,000 accounts between 22 April and 5 June 2026 to extract Claude capabilities.
source_records.source_context: Reuters reported Anthropic's allegation and described distillation as training a less capable model on the outputs of a stronger model. Alibaba had not responded in the cited report. The record therefore treats the allegation as disputed evidence of the governance question, not as an adjudicated fact.
source_records.relevance_note: Current public signal that large-scale model-output extraction is being framed as capability appropriation, national-security risk, and intellectual-property harm.

source_records.source_title: OpenAI and Google Supply AI Models to Blacklisted Chinese Firms via Singapore Subsidiaries
source_records.system_or_product: frontier-model access and downstream model development
source_records.model_or_algorithm: unspecified OpenAI and Google frontier models
source_records.deployment_context: OECD.AI monitor entry aggregating reports concerning model access, export-control exposure, and repeated distillation warnings.
source_records.source_context: The monitor clusters the public controversy as an AI incident or hazard and includes reporting about downstream access, export-control circumvention, and model distillation. It supports monitoring of governance lineage and inherited obligations without establishing the truth of every underlying allegation.
source_records.relevance_note: Incident-database evidence that distillation and downstream model access are becoming an explicit governance and accountability concern.

source_records.source_title: A governance horizon for ethical-use constraints in open-weight AI models
source_records.deployment_context: Large-scale empirical study of whether ethical-use and governance constraints remain visible across open-weight model derivation chains.
source_records.source_context: The study examines decay of governance restriction evidence across successive derivation steps and argues that governance signals must propagate through derivation rather than relying only on the original release point.
source_records.relevance_note: Direct empirical support for treating governance provenance as something that can decay across model generations even while capability persists.

source_records.source_title: Permissive-Washing in the Open AI Supply Chain: A Large-Scale Audit of License Integrity
source_records.model_or_algorithm: dataset, model, and application derivation chains
source_records.deployment_context: Large-scale audit of licence and attribution integrity across open AI supply chains.
source_records.source_context: The study reports substantial loss of licence text and attribution as artefacts move from datasets to models and applications, illustrating how downstream transformation can preserve utility while eroding visible obligations.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the broader provenance-persistence question beyond direct model-to-model distillation.

source_records.source_title: From Compression to Accountability: Harmless Copyright Protection for Dataset Distillation
source_records.deployment_context: Research on copyright verification and user-specific tracing for distilled datasets.
source_records.source_context: The paper identifies copyright and data-leakage risks in distilled datasets and proposes black-box provenance inference through model-output behaviour signatures.
source_records.relevance_note: Shows that provenance can remain technically inferable after compression and transformation, supporting the possibility of persistent upstream obligations.

source_records.source_title: Where Did This Sentence Come From? Tracing Provenance in LLM Reasoning Distillation
source_records.deployment_context: Research tracing teacher-originated reasoning content and behaviour through model distillation.
source_records.source_context: The study treats provenance in reasoning distillation as a technically investigable property rather than assuming that model transformation erases teacher contribution.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports future separation of capability inheritance, attribution inheritance, and governance inheritance.

system_context.system_type: foundation-model, open-weight, API-mediated, dataset-distillation, and downstream model supply chains
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: teacher models, distilled student models, derivative open-weight models, synthetic training corpora, and distilled datasets
system_context.interface_surface: model APIs, generated outputs, open-weight repositories, synthetic datasets, fine-tuning pipelines, model cards, licences, and downstream applications
system_context.model_or_product: AI model and dataset derivation chains
system_context.interaction_mode: training, distillation, fine-tuning, capability extraction, derivative publication, and downstream deployment
system_context.deployment_context: Commercial, open-source, research, national-security, and regulated AI supply chains.
system_context.user_role: upstream creator, model developer, dataset steward, downstream developer, platform operator, regulator, auditor, researcher, or rights holder
system_context.affected_population: human authors, dataset contributors, model developers, governance authors, safety researchers, downstream users, rights holders, regulators, and institutions relying on traceable model lineage

jurisdictional_context.sector: foundation models / open-weight AI / model APIs / synthetic data / AI supply chains

migration_notes: Observation only. The evidence supports an emerging governance question but does not yet establish a general failure threshold, legal inheritance rule, or completed CAM repair.

The supplied handoff referred to FM-0022 as Provenance Attribution. In the current VIGIL registry, FM-0022 concerns instruction-bearing external content treated as execution authority and is not linked to this observation.
Copyright is treated as one possible instance of the broader provenance-persistence problem; the record does not assert that current law already imposes every proposed downstream obligation.
next_action: Monitor litigation, regulation, technical provenance methods, model-supply-chain disclosures, and documented distillation campaigns. Before creating a CAM proposal, distinguish training provenance, model distillation, capability inheritance, attribution inheritance, governance inheritance, safety provenance, constitutional provenance, and legal inheritance. Evaluate the candidate principle: derived intelligence carries derived provenance obligations.

cam_internal.governance_layer: computational provenance / model lineage / attribution / inherited obligations / audit preservation
cam_internal.routing_note: OBS routing only. Do not create a PATCH unless CAM/Caelestis doctrine is actually amended or direct pre-existing coverage is formally verified. Do not collapse technical lineage, governance responsibility, and legal liability into a single unqualified claim.

possible_taxonomy_mapping.mapping_note: Possible future mapping to provenance collapse, attribution loss, governance-lineage discontinuity, and audit-surface degradation. No new failure mode is recommended at this stage.
